title: Arthroscopic devices and methods
abstract: A tissue treatment device has a shaft assembly including an outer sleeve and an inner sleeve. The inner sleeve is co-axially and rotatably received in an axial passageway in the outer sleeve. A dielectric housing has an outer cutting window forming a distal portion of the outer sleeve, and a distal portion of the inner sleeve forms an RF electrode and has an inner cutting window formed therein. The outer and inner cutting windows have outer and inner cutting edges disposed to close together as the inner sleeve is rotated relative to the outer sleeve.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A tissue treatment device, comprising: a shaft assembly including an outer sleeve and an inner sleeve co-axially and rotatably received in an axial passageway of the outer sleeve; a dielectric housing having an outer cutting window forming a distal portion of the outer sleeve; a distal portion of the inner sleeve forming an RF electrode and having an inner cutting window formed therein; said outer and inner cutting windows having outer and inner cutting edges disposed to close together as the inner sleeve is rotated relative to the outer sleeve. 2. The tissue treatment device of claim 1 wherein the dielectric comprises at least one of a ceramic, a glass and a polymer. 3. The tissue treatment device of claim 1 wherein the dielectric comprises a ceramic selected from the group consisting of alumina, zirconia, silicon nitride, yttria- stabilized zirconia, magnesia-stabilized zirconia, ceria-stabilized zirconia and zirconia toughened alumina. 4. The tissue treatment device of claim 1 further comprising; a motor configured to selectively rotate in the inner sleeve in first and second rotational directions; and a radiofrequency (RF) source configured to couple to the RF electrode. 5. The tissue treatment device of claim 4 further comprising a controller operatively coupled to the motor and to the RF source. 6. The tissue treatment device of claim 5 wherein the inner sleeve can be rotated to at least a window-closed position wherein the inner window is covered by a wall of the dielectric housing and to a window-open position wherein the inner window is aligned with the outer window in the dielectric housing and wherein the controller is configured to stop the motor to position the inner sleeve in at least one of the window-open and the window-closed positions. 7. The tissue treatment device of claim 6 wherein the controller is configured to selectively operate the motor and the RF source in at least one of a first mode, a second mode, a third mode, and a fourth mode, wherein (1) the controller operates the motor to rotate or oscillate the inner sleeve without energizing the RF source for mechanically cutting tissue in the first mode, (2) the controller operates the motor to rotate or oscillate the inner sleeve and energizes the RF source for electrosurgically cutting tissue in the second 

 mode, (3) the controller energizes the RF source while the inner sleeve is stationary in the window-closed position to apply coagulative or ablative energy to tissue through the RF electrode in the third mode, and (4) the controller energizes the RF source while the inner sleeve is stationary in the window-open position to apply coagulative or ablative energy to tissue the fourth mode. 8. The tissue treatment device of claim 6 further comprising an opening in a surface of the inner sleeve opposing the inner window for allowing fluid outflow therethrough when the inner sleeve is in the window-closed position. 9. A tissue resecting system comprising: a shaft assembly comprising an elongated outer sleeve with a distal ceramic portion having a resecting window that opens to a bore therein; and an inner sleeve with a distal portion having an inner resecting window and being adapted to rotate in the bore through window-open and window-closed positions to thereby resect tissue engaged by the resecting windows, wherein said distal portion of the inner sleeve is at least partially electrically conductive to form an RF electrode. 10. The tissue resecting system of claim 9 further comprising a motor configured to rotatably drive the inner sleeve relative to the outer sleeve. 11. The tissue resecting system of claim 10 further comprising an RF source operatively coupled to the RF electrode. 12. The tissue resecting system of claim 11 further comprising a negative pressure source coupled to a proximal end of the bore. 13. The tissue resecting system of claim 12 further comprising a controller configured to control operating parameters of the motor, the RF source and the negative pressure source. 14. The tissue resecting system of claim 13 further comprising a hub attached to a proximal end of the shaft assembly, wherein the hub is configured for detachable coupling to a handle carrying the motor. 15. The tissue resecting system of claim 9 further comprising an opening in a surface of the inner sleeve opposing the inner resecting window. 16. A method for treating tissue in a patient, said method comprising: engaging a distal end of a shaft of a tissue treatment device connected to a drive motor and an RF source against a tissue target site submerged in a conductive fluid; and sequentially performing at least two of the following three tissue treatment operations: 

 (1) operating the drive motor to rotate or oscillate an inner sleeve of the shaft without energizing RF source to rotate an inner cutting window on the inner sleeve to mechanically cut tissue at the tissue target site, (2) operating the drive motor to rotate or oscillate the inner sleeve to rotate or oscillate the inner cutting window on the inner sleeve and energizing the RF source to deliver a cutting current for electrosurgically cutting tissue at the tissue target site, and (3) energizing the RF source to deliver a coagulation current to tissue at the tissue target site through a RF electrode on the inner sleeve the RF electrode while the inner sleeve is stationary. 17. The method of claim 16 further comprising sequentially performing all three of the three treatment operations. 18. The method of claim 16 wherein the inner sleeve of the shaft is rotated or oscillated relative to an outer shaft having an outer cutting window so that the inner and outer cutting windows rotate or oscillate past each other to mechanically shear tissue extending through the windows when the windows are aligned without energizing the RF source. 19. The method of claim 16 wherein the inner sleeve of the shaft is rotated or oscillated relative to an outer shaft having an outer cutting window so that the inner and outer cutting windows rotate or oscillate past each other to electrosurgically cut tissue extending through the windows when the windows are aligned while energizing the RF source to apply a cutting current. 20. The method of claim 16 further comprising actuating a negative pressure source in communication with a passageway in the inner sleeve to aspirate conductive fluid and tissue debris from the tissue target site.
